jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer's car crashes during opening sequence
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer D'oh! reaction to mishap
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase in opening crash - perfect character integration and economy but predictable

jokes.text: Roger Corman's Titanic
jokes.setup: Announcement of a 'Thousand Dollar Movie' conclusion
jokes.punchline: It's Roger Corman (notorious B-movie director) doing Titanic
jokes.explanation: Perfect pop culture mashup - Corman's low-budget style applied to Titanic disaster. Economy of words creates immediate absurd visual.

jokes.text: Now I can relax and take off this stifling bikini
jokes.setup: Romantic rescue scene from disaster movie
jokes.punchline: Male character wants to take off his bikini
jokes.explanation: Cross-dressing gag fits Corman B-movie aesthetic. Simple gender reversal for cheap laugh.

jokes.text: Well, I'd rather get a message than see another lousy commercial
jokes.punchline: Homer prefers 'messages' over commercials (not realizing they're the same)
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer density - missing obvious while being righteously indignant. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Bi-Mon-Sci-Fi-Con
jokes.punchline: Awkward abbreviation sounds like 'Bi-Mon'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay on convention naming conventions. Memorable but not exceptional.

jokes.text: the gay robots of Star Wars
jokes.punchline: C-3PO and R2-D2 described as 'gay robots'
jokes.explanation: Dated gay joke that relies more on shock than clever observation. Economical but cheap.

jokes.text: Oh, you stupid little tramp. You're so boring. I hate you!
jokes.punchline: C-3PO trash-talking R2-D2 in combat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of C-3PO's polite character into aggressive wrestler persona. Character-specific and unexpected.

jokes.text: Well, beats work / Yeah, beats school
jokes.punchline: Father-son bonding over avoiding responsibilities
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Homer-Bart parallel attitudes. Not groundbreaking but well-executed.

jokes.text: Are you sure this is a sci-fi convention? It's full of nerds
jokes.punchline: Homer surprised that sci-fi convention has nerds
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - stating the obvious as if it's surprising. Great character density moment.

jokes.text: Hi, Lisa / Hey, Lisa / Guten Tag
jokes.punchline: Multiple nerds know Lisa personally, including international greeting
jokes.explanation: Establishes Lisa's nerd credentials through social connections. The 'Guten Tag' escalates the joke nicely.

jokes.text: [Weakly] Hi.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's embarrassed, weak response
jokes.explanation: Perfect embarrassed reaction beat showing Lisa's social discomfort

jokes.text: This is one small step towards firing your ass!
jokes.setup: Real astronaut trying to get attention
jokes.punchline: Neil Armstrong quote twisted into workplace threat
jokes.explanation: Clever reworking of famous quote into modern workplace context. Good structure and memorable.

jokes.text: Oh, the pain, the pain. The pain of it all
jokes.punchline: Dr. Smith's catchphrase delivery
jokes.explanation: Straight reference without much twist. Works for nostalgia but lacks originality.

jokes.text: Silence, you nickel-plated nitwit!
jokes.punchline: Another Dr. Smith catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Another straight reference. The alliterative insult has good rhythm but no surprise.

jokes.text: Danger, danger, Bart Simpson
jokes.punchline: Robot warns about comic book mixing instead of actual danger
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of Robot's warning system for trivial nerd concern. Solid misdirection.

jokes.text: A, that is not water. It is diet Mr. Pibb. And B, I- ooh
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's pedantic correction interrupted by attraction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment - pedantic detail derailed by human attraction. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Comb the Sweet Tarts out of your beard and you're on
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy asking about dating prospects
jokes.punchline: Hygiene requirement involving candy debris in beard
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific gross-out detail that perfectly captures nerd stereotype. Very quotable and visual.

jokes.text: Don't try to change me, baby
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's delusional romantic confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Comic Book Guy's delusional self-image. Great contrast with his gross reality.

jokes.text: As you can see, you stand to save up to 17 cents a month over the more dependable providers
jokes.punchline: Mark Hamill shilling for Sprint instead of talking Star Wars
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of celebrity endorsements and fan disappointment. Meta-commentary on commercialization.

jokes.text: You stupid nerds! He's trying to save you money on long distance
jokes.setup: Crowd booing Mark Hamill's Sprint pitch
jokes.punchline: Homer defending the commercial as if it's generous
jokes.explanation: Homer missing the point entirely and defending corporate shilling. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: My whole life has been leading to this moment
jokes.setup: Mark Hamill asking for Obi-Wan volunteer
jokes.punchline: Fan's overly dramatic life investment in roleplay opportunity
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing fan obsession. Not particularly original but well-executed.

jokes.text: With the acting and the groupies and the 'Luke, Luke, save me' with the light saber and the 'vwing, vwing, vwing'
jokes.setup: Fan explaining why he deserves the role
jokes.punchline: Incoherent rambling with made-up lightsaber sound effects
jokes.explanation: Captures fan delusion and poor articulation. The 'vwing' sound effect is memorably specific.

jokes.text: That's not how you 'vwing'
jokes.punchline: Another fan correcting the sound effect technique
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing nerd pedantry about imaginary sound effects. Brilliant economy and specificity.

jokes.text: Oh, God. Can't this town go one day without a riot?
jokes.punchline: Mayor's weary acceptance of Springfield's riot tendency
jokes.explanation: Good running gag about Springfield's chaos. Character-appropriate exasperation from the mayor.

jokes.text: Ya! Gadzooks! Where are my bodyguards?
jokes.setup: Mayor realizing he's in danger from riot
jokes.punchline: Archaic exclamation followed by panic about protection
jokes.explanation: Good Quimby character voice with old-fashioned exclamation

jokes.text: Is there anything fluffier than a cloud? If there is, I don't wanna know about it
jokes.punchline: Lenny's completely unrelated philosophical cloud observation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly random observation in middle of chaos. Perfect Lenny character moment - profoundly simple yet oddly profound.

jokes.text: You know, George Lucas makes me pay for these
jokes.setup: Fans demanding he use his lightsaber
jokes.punchline: Mark Hamill reveals he has to pay for prop damage
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about actor's relationship with franchise creator. Reveals the business side behind the fantasy.

jokes.text: Not the hair! Back off, you freakin' dweebs
jokes.punchline: Mark Hamill's vanity about his hair during mob attack
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing actor vanity in crisis. The hair concern humanizes celebrity fear.

jokes.text: Nerds!
jokes.setup: Homer seeing Mark Hamill in danger
jokes.punchline: Homer's war cry against nerds
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer battle cry - treating nerds as mortal enemies with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Homer Simpson, nerd-buster
jokes.punchline: Ghostbusters parody title for nerd-fighting
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture mashup. Works but relies on reference humor rather than original concept.

jokes.text: Never! [saves Mark Hamill]
jokes.setup: Mark Hamill saying to leave him behind
jokes.punchline: Homer's overly dramatic heroic refusal
jokes.explanation: Standard heroic parody moment. Homer gets to be the hero but it's fairly predictable.

jokes.text: How was I supposed to know it's not a real spaceship?
jokes.punchline: Bart's defense of trying to pilot convention display
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - reasonable excuse for unreasonable action. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Forget Maggie. She's gone / I've got Maggie
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately reveals she has Maggie
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Homer's tendency to give up too quickly. Dark humor about abandoning baby.

jokes.text: Hey! I thought you twisted your ankle
jokes.setup: Mark Hamill claimed injury to avoid danger
jokes.punchline: Homer notices he's walking fine now
jokes.explanation: Standard fake injury reveal. Competent but predictable setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: Who else you gonna find to take a bullet for you? Or have his genitals hooked up to a car battery?
jokes.punchline: Listing increasingly disturbing job requirements
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from standard bodyguard duty to torture. Dark humor with good shock value.

jokes.text: I'll tell you who. Him!
jokes.setup: Bodyguard asking who else would do the job
jokes.punchline: Mayor pointing at Homer
jokes.explanation: Functional plot setup with Homer as unlikely choice

jokes.text: Whoo-hoo! / I said, 'Whoo-hoo.'
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiastic acceptance despite obvious warning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - completely missing danger while being enthusiastic. The repeat emphasizes his obliviousness.

jokes.text: Not to your family. Not to your country. Not to 'Moo-hammad.'
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing Muhammad as cow sound
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that relies on pronunciation humor. Not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: Even during Ramadan? Shut your sass-hole, boy.
jokes.punchline: Homer's cultural question and instructor's harsh response
jokes.explanation: Homer showing unexpected cultural awareness followed by aggressive shutdown

jokes.text: These melons represent your protectees
jokes.punchline: Using fruit as stand-ins for important people
jokes.explanation: Decent absurd training setup. Visual gag that works but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: [Slurping] What?
jokes.punchline: Homer eating his 'protectee'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - complete failure to understand metaphor and eating the thing he's supposed to protect

jokes.text: [Homer eating the melon while instructor talks]
jokes.setup: Melons representing important people to protect
jokes.punchline: Homer eating his 'protectee' during lecture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - missing the point while following his appetite. Great visual comedy.

jokes.text: Well, your dive wasn't bad, but I just didn't believe your 'No!'
jokes.punchline: Acting criticism during violence training
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke treating violence like performance art. Good blend of action and acting criticism.

jokes.text: Remember, your 'No!' is what gets you your next job
jokes.punchline: Career advice about dramatic death scenes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - treating death as a career opportunity. Very quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Now drop and give me 20. / No! / Better.
jokes.punchline: Homer applies acting advice to refusing exercise
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with perfect economy. Homer applies lesson in unexpected context with instructor's approval.

jokes.text: Not one of you fit to guard a Russian rock band
jokes.punchline: Using Russian rock band as lowest possible standard
jokes.explanation: Good specific insult implying Russian rock bands are lowest-status clients. Cultural reference with good specificity.

jokes.text: However, your checks have cleared, so you all graduate. Congratulations.
jokes.punchline: Graduation based solely on payment, not skill
jokes.explanation: Excellent cynical humor about for-profit training - completely undermines credibility for money

jokes.text: The pig is in the poke
jokes.punchline: Mangling common expression into nonsense
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer malapropism. Works for character but not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: I really don't care for that code name
jokes.setup: Homer's pig reference about the mayor
jokes.punchline: Quimby's dignified objection to being called a pig
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Quimby's vanity. Simple but effective reaction beat.

jokes.text: Look out, Marge! Cat!
jokes.setup: Homer being overly protective as bodyguard
jokes.punchline: Overreacting to harmless household cat
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy showing Homer's misplaced vigilance. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Hold it. What's your clearance? / We just wanna get a snack. / Access denied.
jokes.setup: Homer taking security too seriously at home
jokes.punchline: Denying his own children kitchen access
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's job obsession affecting family life. Character-driven absurdity.

jokes.text: They'll be fine in half an hour
jokes.setup: Marge objecting to Homer using sleeper hold on kids
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal of temporarily unconscious children
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about child safety with Homer's characteristic casual attitude toward violence.

jokes.text: Quick, honk at that broad
jokes.setup: Mayor discussing dignity and proper behavior
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking for sexist car harassment
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy moment. Shows gap between Quimby's public and private persona.

jokes.text: Homer, why didn't you say you was with the mayor? Shove off, pukeholes!
jokes.punchline: Complete attitude reversal upon seeing mayor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moe's opportunistic nature. The insult 'pukeholes' is memorably gross.

jokes.text: Mmm, semi-imported
jokes.punchline: Homer's made-up category for mediocre beer
jokes.explanation: Good Homer food observation. 'Semi-imported' is a perfectly Homer way to describe fake quality.

jokes.text: We're working on that roach situation. I swear to God.
jokes.punchline: Moe's nervous admission about health violations
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out humor about restaurant cleanliness. Works but not particularly original.

jokes.text: We're working on that roach situation. I swear to God.
jokes.setup: Mayor's veiled reference to health inspection
jokes.punchline: Moe's panicked promise about obvious health violations
jokes.explanation: Standard Moe's Tavern health violation humor

jokes.text: And I ordered a double-double, but they gave me the double-double-double-double
jokes.punchline: Increasingly complex burger order nomenclature
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist escalation of fast food naming. Shows Homer's food obsession over corruption.

jokes.text: That 'change' was a bribe
jokes.setup: Homer's innocent description of mayor's day
jokes.punchline: Lisa stating the obvious corruption
jokes.explanation: Straightforward exposition moment. Lisa as voice of reason works but isn't comedically exceptional.

jokes.text: Sure the mayor takes a few bribes, but he also makes the trains run on time
jokes.punchline: Homer defending corruption with fascist efficiency argument
jokes.explanation: Clever historical reference to Mussolini defense mixed with Homer's moral flexibility. Good political satire.

jokes.text: No, he doesn't. Trains are regulated by the federal Department of Transportation
jokes.punchline: Lisa's pedantic factual correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - derailing political argument with bureaucratic facts. Smart character writing.

jokes.text: However, in the future I would prefer a nondescript briefcase to the sack with a dollar sign on it
jokes.setup: Mayor accepting bribe from Fat Tony
jokes.punchline: Requesting more subtle bribe packaging
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy premise - cartoonish money sack in realistic corruption. Great contrast between style and substance.

jokes.text: Hey, I called that. I saw it first. Come back here.
jokes.punchline: Claiming dibs on appetizers like a child
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character comedy - reverting to childish behavior at serious meeting

jokes.text: Crap on a crust!
jokes.punchline: Homer's food-based profanity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer-specific swear word that's both clean and character-appropriate. Very quotable food-based curse.

jokes.text: Rats? I'm outraged. You promised me dog or higher
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony's quality standards for unethical milk
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - establishing hierarchy of unethical animal products. 'Dog or higher' is memorably absurd.

jokes.text: Hey! My milk. I traded my math book for that
jokes.setup: Homer stopping kids from drinking contaminated milk
jokes.punchline: Nelson's misplaced priorities in education vs. milk
jokes.explanation: Good Nelson character moment showing his warped value system. Education vs. milk trade-off is absurd but character-specific.

jokes.text: Can I still drink it? / Go nuts
jokes.setup: Homer warning kids about contaminated milk
jokes.punchline: Homer allowing Bart to drink poison because he asked
jokes.explanation: Dark parenting humor showing Homer's casual attitude toward child safety. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: You wouldn't even be alive if it wasn't for me!
jokes.setup: Quimby criticizing Homer after corruption discovery
jokes.punchline: Homer's indignant reminder of life-saving
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's sense of being underappreciated. Standard but effective.

jokes.text: All right, stay calm. I'll just use the body to stage an elaborate farce a la Weekend at Bernie's
jokes.setup: Homer thinking he killed the mayor
jokes.punchline: Immediate plan to use corpse for comedy scheme
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference with dark humor. Homer's movie solution to real problem is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: His corpse is climbing the building / I'm alive, you idiot
jokes.punchline: Mayor revealing he's alive while Homer narrates climbing
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection gag with good structure. Competent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Did you know that this is how F.D.R. met Eleanor?
jokes.setup: Mayor hanging from building looking into windows
jokes.punchline: False historical claim about presidential romance
jokes.explanation: Good absurd historical reference showing Quimby's sleazy nature even in danger. Creative false fact.

jokes.text: Did you know that this is how F.D.R. met Eleanor?
jokes.punchline: Absurd historical claim about presidential romance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd historical claim combining creepy behavior with presidential history

jokes.text: Six queens. / Not so fast. Seven queens.
jokes.setup: Card game with apparent winning hand
jokes.punchline: Impossible card count revealing cheating
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist card game logic. Simple math-based impossibility humor with good economy.

jokes.text: Six queens. Read 'em and weep. Not so fast. Seven queens.
jokes.setup: Poker game with impossible six queens
jokes.punchline: Even more impossible seven queens
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of impossible poker hands showing mob cheating

jokes.text: Right, right. Filthy I-talian-Americans
jokes.punchline: Correcting to politically correct version while keeping 'filthy'
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum stupidity moment - fixing terminology while missing the point about prejudice.

jokes.text: The mayor's office is not for sale
jokes.punchline: Sudden declaration of integrity
jokes.explanation: Standard character arc moment. Works for story but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: Can you, uh, edit out the laughs?
jokes.setup: Mobsters laughing at mayor's ethical declaration
jokes.punchline: Mayor asking camera crew to edit out humiliation
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor breaking fourth wall with mayor's awareness of being filmed

jokes.text: Can you, uh, edit out the laughs?
jokes.setup: Mobsters laughing at mayor's integrity declaration
jokes.punchline: Mayor asking to edit reality like a TV show
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke treating reality like television production. Very quotable and clever concept.

jokes.text: I am not so much disappointed as I am blinded with rage
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of violent anger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fat Tony character voice - polite phrasing for violent emotions. Great contrast and quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, what are you gonna do, call your godfather, huh? 'Oh, Godfather'-
jokes.punchline: Homer's terrible Godfather impression interrupted by fear
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment with bad impression cut short by realization of danger

jokes.text: Oh, what are you gonna do, call your godfather, huh? 'Oh, Godfather'
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's fear when he sees the actual godfather
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Wiggum's false bravado. Standard structure but well-executed.

jokes.text: Run free, little vermin. The city is yours.
jokes.punchline: Treating rat release as liberation rather than infestation
jokes.explanation: Good ironic perspective on environmental consequence. Treats pest release as noble act.

jokes.text: the circle of life is complete.
jokes.punchline: Overly philosophical news commentary
jokes.explanation: Standard pretentious news commentary parody

jokes.text: Everyone loves rats, but they don't wanna drink the rats' milk?
jokes.setup: Fat Tony's business being shut down
jokes.punchline: Confused logic about rat appreciation vs. consumption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic about consumer preferences. Fat Tony's genuine confusion is very quotable.

jokes.text: if I were Fat Tony and, God willing, some day I will be
jokes.setup: Bart commenting on Fat Tony's situation
jokes.punchline: Bart's career aspiration to become a mobster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment - casual criminal aspiration. Dark humor with good character consistency.

jokes.text: Oh, don't worry about that, boy. He's already out on bail
jokes.setup: Bart worried about Fat Tony in jail getting angrier
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual revelation that the threat is immediate
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness to danger. Makes threat worse while trying to be reassuring.

jokes.text: Bart, would you go start Daddy's car? Homer!
jokes.setup: Homer preparing to leave house despite mob threats
jokes.punchline: Asking child to start potentially booby-trapped car
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with Homer's casual child endangerment and Marge's horrified reaction

jokes.text: I was thinking of a quiet evening at home with the, uh- [sighs] wife
jokes.setup: Mayor explaining why he wants to stay home
jokes.punchline: Quimby's visible struggle to pretend to care about his wife
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Quimby's martial discomfort. The hesitation and sigh sell the joke.

jokes.text: Yes! / Why do you talk that way? / I had a stroke.
jokes.punchline: Medical explanation for what seemed like affectation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark misdirection. Homer's rude question gets unexpectedly serious answer. Great economy.

jokes.text: All right, Fat Tony. If it's trouble with Quimby you're after- / Please. I am only here as a devotee of the lively arts
jokes.setup: Homer confronting Fat Tony at theater
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony's cultured excuse for being at musical
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Fat Tony's sophisticated cultural interests vs. murder plans.

jokes.text: You moron! That's the kiss of death
jokes.punchline: Mayor explaining the mob significance Homer missed
jokes.explanation: Standard cultural reference joke. Works but relies on mob movie knowledge rather than original humor.

jokes.text: You moron! That's the kiss of death. Oh, no! Wait. Maybe I didn't do it right.
jokes.setup: Homer delivering Fat Tony's message with kiss
jokes.punchline: Mayor's explanation followed by Homer's hope he messed up death threat
jokes.explanation: Good cultural misunderstanding with Homer's hopeful incompetence

jokes.text: Ah, au gratin potatoes. That's a quality side.
jokes.setup: Homer suspicious of assassination attempt via food
jokes.punchline: Homer's food appreciation overriding paranoia
jokes.explanation: Good Homer food moment. His appreciation for cuisine overrides safety concerns in character-appropriate way.

jokes.text: Nathan Detroit would never wear this. And this song isn't even in the show
jokes.setup: Mark Hamill backstage at community theater
jokes.punchline: Professional actor's frustration with amateur production quality
jokes.explanation: Good theater humor showing professional vs. amateur standards. Meta-commentary on community theater quality.

jokes.text: I don't have time for this. I got 75 shortcakes to strawberry.
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing catering duties over artistic concerns
jokes.explanation: Great celebrity comedy showing Mark Hamill's post-Star Wars career reality

jokes.text: I want the mayor dead. I want his wife dead. I want his cat and his dog dead
jokes.setup: Fat Tony ordering hit on mayor
jokes.punchline: Extending murder list to include pets
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Fat Tony's thoroughness. Absurd inclusion of pets in murder list.

jokes.text: Wait, wait, wait, wait. Who is before the cat?
jokes.punchline: Henchman's confusion about hit order rather than morality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - treating murder list like grocery list. Focus on logistics rather than ethics.

jokes.text: Just kill the mayor. Y-You're not mad at me, are you?
jokes.punchline: Louie's vulnerable question about boss's feelings
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy with hitman's emotional insecurity

jokes.text: But I'm just here to kill- [gets pulled into dance number]
jokes.setup: Professional killer trying to complete assassination
jokes.punchline: Being forced to participate in musical theater
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist premise - murder interrupted by musical obligation. Great contrast between violence and performance.

jokes.text: But I'm just here to kill-
jokes.setup: Louie being forced into musical number
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing assassination mission
jokes.explanation: Standard interrupted confession gag

jokes.text: I think I saw him in Rent or Stomp or Clomp or some piece of crap
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive attitude toward theater with made-up show name
jokes.explanation: Good Homer anti-culture moment. 'Clomp' as made-up show name is funny but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You never even finished Jedi school
jokes.punchline: Assassin's Star Wars-based insult during combat
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference. Works in context but relies on franchise knowledge rather than original wit.

jokes.text: The Force? / The forks. Use the forks.
jokes.setup: Mark Hamill expecting Star Wars reference
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical suggestion about dinner utensils
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect setup. Homer's practical interpretation of mystical concept. Great economy and structure.

jokes.text: Nobody messes with the mayor when Homer Simpson's on- Oh, crap.
jokes.setup: Homer's heroic declaration after defeating assassin
jokes.punchline: Boast interrupted by seeing Fat Tony with weapon
jokes.explanation: Standard interrupted boast with Homer's confidence immediately deflated

jokes.text: I'd say you were luminous, magnetic. Incandescent.
jokes.setup: Mark Hamill praising his own performance
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate self-congratulation
jokes.explanation: Standard actor vanity joke. Escalation works but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: Shoot. I forgot my light saber. Oh, wait. Here it is.
jokes.setup: Mark Hamill needing to fight fans again
jokes.punchline: Conveniently finding his prop weapon
jokes.explanation: Weak ending gag that relies on convenient prop discovery. Not particularly clever or memorable.
